response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates exceptional engineering maturity and 20 years of full-stack experience, his technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with this C++ engineering role. His C++ work is relegated to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a 2009 thesis; his current and recent expertise (2018–2025) centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang in SaaS/fintech contexts, not industrial automation. The role requires modern C++ depth, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, and IoT/MQTT/industrial-protocol familiarity—none of which appear on his resume. He is overqualified as a full-stack engineer but underqualified for this specific C++-focused, industrial-domain position.
strengths: Extensive full-stack engineering maturity with 20 years of experience and demonstrated software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, architecture thinking)
Strong Node.js, TypeScript, and React expertise—three of the listed advantageous skills—plus hands-on REST API and event-driven system design
Track record of technical leadership, mentoring, and open-source contributions (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts) showing communication and ownership
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill; most recent C++ work was 2014–2015 at National Instruments; current role (2025) and recent 7 years focus entirely on TypeScript/Node.js/DLang

No exposure to industrial domain (IEC 62443, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0) or explicit secure-development-lifecycle (SDL) implementation; background is web/SaaS/fintech, not automation machinery or IoT
Education is BS only; role specifies B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech preferred; no mention of MQTT protocol, formal secure coding certification, or UML/design-pattern documentation experience
